n.description: Number of production lines (independent trials in the binomial model)

k.description: The exact number of lines that use more than 6 thousands, for which the probability is being computed

p.description: Probability that a single production line uses more than 6 thousands on a given day, derived from the exponential distribution with mean 6: P(X > 6) = e^(-1) ≈ 0.3679
